title: The French people's vote is still up for grabs
subhead: The clear and decisive municipal election results in most cities show that the 2027 presidential election is far from a foregone conclusion. While distrust and the democratic malaise are very real, voters are not indifferent to the growth of the radical left and the far right's possible rise to power.

EU speeds up free trade with Mercosur treaty and new Australia agreement
subhead: South America, India and now Australia. European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen welcomed on Monday the 'trade trilogy' that Brussels has built in just a few months to boost the EU's exchanges with the rest of the world despite strong resistance, particularly from the agricultural sector.

title: Lionel Jospin, former French prime minister, dies aged 88
subhead: Jospin, a Socialist who introduced the 35-hour work week and civil partnerships for gay couples, was head of government from 1997 to 2002 before losing to the far right in presidential polls.

title: French municipal elections: Full results in every city, town and village
subhead: French voters elected more than 34,000 mayors on March 15 and 22. Use our search engine to find the winner in any municipality.

title: Paris municipal elections: Emmanuel Grégoire and the united left achieve a resounding victory
subhead: Despite a united front from the entire right-wing and centrist parties – as well as competition on the left with La France Insoumise candidate Sophia Chikirou remaining in the race – the Socialist candidate delivered the united left its most impressive victory in Paris since 2001.
byline: Hélène Bekmezian,  Léo Lagniez and  Raphaëlle Bacqué

title: Is anti-scooter sentiment fueled by class contempt?
subhead: Electric scooters are booming in popularity, but local officials are increasingly moving to restrict or ban them. The backlash raises a broader question: Are scooters the future of urban transport or a growing urban irritant?

title: France's aircraft carrier located in real time by Le Monde through Strava data
subhead: As the Charles de Gaulle and its strike group approach the Middle East, Le Monde identified a French sailor using the Strava fitness application in the Mediterranean Sea. This security flaw remains unaddressed despite our previous revelations.
